flights.outbound: SAS SK4744/4438 · BRU 17:05 → Oslo → Tromsø, land 22:50 (Sat 1 Aug)
flights.return: SAS SK4453/0597 · TOS 15:00 → Copenhagen → BRU, home 19:50 (Fri 14 Aug)

timedAnchors.what: Melbu–Fiskebøl ferry into Lofoten (~hourly, pay on board)
Fly home · TOS → CPH → BRU, land 19:50

blocks.text: Land in Tromsø on SK 4744 / 4438 and check in — first taste of the never-quite-dark Arctic night.
A full day to acclimatise: ride the Fjellheisen cable car (or climb the Sherpa steps) for the view over the island city, cross to the Arctic Cathedral, and wander the harbour and Polaria.
Shops shut on Sunday, so just grab essentials — the big provisioning run waits for Monday.
Collect the camper and roll into Day 1.

deepdive.lead: You land at 22:50 with the sun still up. Nothing to do but drop the bags and let the town do the rest.

route.summary: No driving — you’re on foot in Tromsø until the van pickup on Monday.

legs.note: Flybussen or taxi; the airport is on Tromsøya, minutes from the centre.

sleep.name: Tromsø · 2 nights in town
sleep.note: Two easy nights before the van. Budget line carries kr 4,200 (≈ €375) — still a placeholder until you book.

hikes.name: Fjellheisen · Storsteinen
hikes.note: Cable car up to 421 m for the city-and-fjord view. Or walk the 1,200 Sherpa steps beside it if legs are itching (~45 min up).
hikes.stats: cable car · 4 min · or ~45 min on foot

eat.name: Fiskekompaniet
eat.note: Harbourside, the best shellfish in town — king crab, oysters, catch of the day. Book ahead; it fills in August.

eat.name: Emmas Drømmekjøkken
eat.note: Across from the cathedral. Tasting menus (5/8/10 courses) of modern northern Norwegian. The blow-out option if you want one.

eat.name: Risø / Kaffebønna
eat.note: Where locals actually take their coffee. Good for a slow first morning.

drink.name: Ølhallen
drink.note: Mack’s own beer hall — Norway’s oldest brewery, 1877. ~70 taps, closes early-ish. Do this one.

drink.name: Bastard Bar
drink.note: Cocktails and a late crowd if the light keeps you up.

tips: The sun barely sets — the midnight-sun window has just closed but it stays bright past midnight. Bring the eye mask.
Do the big shop tomorrow, not today: no van, no fridge.
Vinmonopolet (state liquor store) is the only place for wine/spirits over 4.7% — Mon–Fri to 18:00, Sat to 15:00, closed Sunday. Stock the van here, it’s far cheaper than the islands.

days.title: Tromsø → Senja

blocks.text: Pick up the camper at Arctic Campers in Kvaløysletta — a quick walk-through of the van, then load up.
Big grocery shop in Tromsø while the supermarkets are finally open after Sunday — stock the van for the days ahead.
Drive west toward Senja, pulling in at Tungeneset's wind-carved rocks and the postcard beach at Ersfjord.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Settle at the Fjordgård / Mefjord campsite, cast off the pier and fire up the first BBQ of the trip.

days.noteHTML: Booked: Camp Mefjord — check-in from 14:00. Confirmation is in Gmail.

deepdive.lead: Van day. Collect at Kvaløysletta, do the one big shop of the trip, then drive into Senja’s outer edge.

route.summary: ~250 km, 3h45 of actual driving — but with the pickup and the shop, expect it to eat the day.

legs.note: Pickup 09:00. Walk the van with the handover checklist: gas, water, toilet, tyre kit.
E8 south then Rv858 — the big supermarkets are here, last cheap prices before Senja.
Fv862 over the island. Narrow, twisting, spectacular — don’t rush the last hour.

route.driveTip: Fill the tank in Finnsnes. Fuel on outer Senja is scarce and dear.

sleep.name: Camp Mefjord
sleep.note: Booked ✓ check-in from 14:00. Right on the pier at Mefjordvær.
facilities: Pier access — cast straight from the site

eat.name: The big shop · Coop Extra / Rema 1000, Finnsnes
eat.note: The one real provisioning stop. Coffee, breakfast, pasta/rice, tins, oil, salt, butter, onions, potatoes, beer, snacks. Prices climb the further out you go.

eat.name: Salteriet, Mefjord Brygge
eat.note: Local ‘yttersida’ menu — halibut with grated carrot and browned butter, whale steak, reindeer. Fjord views. 5 min from the campsite if you don’t feel like cooking on arrival night.

eat.name: Pier-side BBQ
eat.note: The plan of record: grill at the pier, beer in hand, midnight light. Buy the fish or catch it.

drink.name: Van stock
drink.note: Whatever you loaded at Vinmonopolet in Tromsø. Beer up to 4.7% is in the supermarkets, but only until 20:00 weekdays / 18:00 Saturdays — and never on Sunday.

tips: Norwegian supermarket beer stops selling at 20:00 sharp on weekdays. Cashiers will not bend.
Download offline maps for Senja now — coverage on the outer fjords is patchy.
Test the gas ring and the fridge before you leave the depot, not at 22:00 on a pier.

days.title: Segla day, stage for the ferry

blocks.text: — an early climb for the trip's signature view, Segla's blade rising straight out of the sea (~4.5–5h).
Back at the van for a long lunch, legs earned.
Take the slow scenic road toward Gryllefjord, stopping at the Bergsbotn viewpoint over the fjord.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: A loose afternoon by the water near the quay — sleep close to the ferry for an easy morning.

deepdive.lead: The trip’s first real summit, then a short reposition to sleep beside the ferry quay.

route.summary: ~70 km, 1h30 — a light driving day built around the hike.

legs.note: Parking fills by 09:00 in season. Paid parking, card only.
Stage right by the quay — you want to be in the queue at 08:30 tomorrow.

sleep.name: Gryllefjord · scenic night by the quay
sleep.note: No campsite — a free/scenic pitch near the ferry quay. Allemannsretten allows a night in the van in a lay-by; be discreet, leave nothing.
facilities: Zero facilities — top up water at the campsite before leaving Mefjord

hikes.note: The postcard: the Segla wall dropping 640 m straight into the fjord, seen from across the ridge. Steep, boggy in places, exposed at the top — trail shoes with grip, not trainers. The view is at the very end; don’t turn back early.

eat.name: Bergsbotn / Tungeneset picnic
eat.note: Two of Senja’s National Tourist Route viewpoints sit on the road to Fjordgård. Coffee and a sandwich at the Tungeneset boardwalk beats any café.

eat.name: Summit lunch
eat.note: Pack it up Hesten — a thermos and a roll at the top with Segla in front of you is one of the meals you’ll remember.

eat.name: Joker Gryllefjord
eat.note: Small village shop for a top-up. Limited stock, early close — don’t rely on it for dinner.

drink.name: Summit thermos
drink.note: Coffee at the top. It’s the whole point.

tips: Check the Gryllefjord ferry queue tonight — cars start lining up the evening before in high season.
Hesten is exposed and unmarked in fog. If the cloud is on the ridge, the view is gone; save it and take the Segla side instead.
Fill the water tank before you leave Camp Mefjord. Tonight has nothing.

days.title: First ferry → Bleik

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Join the Gryllefjord ferry queue with breakfast brewing in the van and a line in the water while you wait.
The ferry sails the open-sea crossing to Andenes (whales are a real chance) — arrive 12:40.
Settle in early at Midnattsol Camping, Bleik, before the afternoon spots fill.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Bleik beach and an optional Måtind walk; mackerel off the pier — or take the boat out to Bleiksøya's puffin colony.

blocks.label: Golf
blocks.text: Tee off at Bleik Golfstrømbane — the 9-hole links is a few hundred metres from the pitch, and the sun only sets at 22:49. It never drops more than 6° below the horizon, so it stays light enough to play right through to the 03:09 sunrise.

days.noteHTML: Fallback: miss the 11:00 and the next sailing is 15:00 — but expect Bleik to be busier by then.

deepdive.lead: The open-sea crossing to Andøya, then a beach and a pier full of mackerel.

route.summary: ~25 km of driving, plus a 1h40 ferry that is the day.

legs.note: Open-sea crossing, arrive 12:40. Stand on deck: whales and sea eagles are a real chance.
Short hop down the west coast to the campsite.

route.driveTip: Miss the 11:00 and the next is 15:00 — which puts you into a much busier Bleik.

sleep.name: Midnattsol Camping, Bleik
sleep.note: No booking — turn up early. Aim to settle by ~13:00, before the afternoon fills. Beach on one side, mountains on the other.
facilities: Steps from Bleik beach and the pier

hikes.name: Måtind
hikes.note: Optional afternoon walk. Boardwalked start, a scramble at the end, and a knife-edge view down the whole 2.5 km of Bleik beach. Do it if the ferry lands you in good weather.

eat.name: Eat before you tee off
eat.note: The clubhouse is a clubhouse, not a kitchen. Grill the mackerel at the pitch around 20:00 so you're on the first tee for 22:30 with the sun on the horizon.

eat.name: Mackerel off the pier
eat.note: August is peak mackerel. A simple spinner or a feather rig off Bleik pier, evening tide. Bleed them, fillet them, straight in the pan with butter and salt. Best meal of the trip if it lands.

eat.name: Café Alveland, Andenes
eat.note: Hand-made cinnamon rolls, cakes and open sandwiches in nostalgic old premises. Open daily in summer.

eat.name: Restaurant Orion, Andenes
eat.note: Best setting in Andenes, down by the water. Reindeer stew, grilled stockfish.

eat.name: Coop Prix Andenes
eat.note: Top-up shop on the way through. Last decent-sized store before Vesterålen.

drink.name: Beach beer
drink.note: Bleik faces west into the low sun. Cold one on the sand, ~22:00, is the assignment.

tips: Enquiry sent ✓ — WhatsApp to the club (+47 974 30 312) on 14 Jul 2026: asked for a ~22:30 tee-off on Wed 5 Aug, and two rental sets — one left-handed (men's) + one right-handed (ladies'). Awaiting reply. The left-handed set is the open question: small nine-hole clubs often carry only a couple of bags, and left-handed is the first thing they don't have. If they can't, bring your own or make it a walk with a beer.

Midnight golf: Bleik Golfstrømbane, 9 holes on the links right by the campsite. Green fee kr 250 for 9, kr 300 for 18, kr 500 for 24-hour play. Clubs kr 200 a set — bring nothing. Ring ahead: +47 974 30 312.

The light, honestly: Bleik's true midnight sun ends 24 July, so on 5 August the sun does set — 22:49, back up 03:09. But it never sinks more than 6° below the horizon, so there's no real darkness: it's continuous twilight and you can read a scorecard at 01:00. Midnight golf, just not midnight-sun golf.
The puffin safari to Bleiksøya (~80,000 pairs, sea eagles overhead, 90 min) leaves from the harbour right by the campsite — book the afternoon you arrive, it often sells out.
Ferry is cash-free; they scan the plate and card on board.
If the mackerel are in, catch more than you need — fillets keep two days in the van fridge.

blocks.text: A slow morning on Andøya — the Bleik beach, the Måtind ridge, or the whale safari out of Andenes if you fancy it. You have until ~12:30.
Roll south through Vesterålen. Stop in Sortland for the last big, cheap restock at Coop Extra — you drive straight through the town either way.
Melbu–Fiskebøl ferry (~25 min, roughly hourly, pay on board). First sight of the Lofoten wall.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Into Lofoten a day early. Find a scenic pitch out toward Laukvik on Austvågøy — a bonus Lofoten evening you weren’t supposed to have.

deepdive.lead: The swap: give up the Vesterålen night and cross into Lofoten a day early. Same slow Andøya morning, one longer drive, and you land an extra Lofoten evening.

route.summary: ~185 km, 3h15 plus a 25-min ferry. The longest day since D1, but still shorter than it — and you drive through Sortland regardless, so the big restock is unchanged.
route.driveTip: Diesel and the Coop Extra shop in Sortland. Aim for a Melbu ferry around 16:00–16:30; they run roughly hourly and you pay on board.

legs.note: Whale safari (back ~13:00 if you take it) or the Space Centre. If you do the safari, push the ferry back an hour — the light holds until 22:30.
Rv82 down Andøya’s east side, over the bridge into Vesterålen. Sortland is ‘the blue town’ — murals, and the last cheap supermarket before Lofoten.
South to the ferry quay.
Frequent sailings, no booking, pay on board. Stand on deck — the Lofoten wall comes up out of the sea.
E10 south, then out to the north coast. Open horizon, and you’re a day ahead of the plan.

sleep.name: Austvågøy · scenic night (near Laukvik)
sleep.note: The night you gained. A free pitch on the north coast, facing the open sea — and it puts you 10 minutes from Sandsletta, where you’re booked tomorrow anyway.
facilities: No services — fill water and empty tanks in Sortland on the way through

eat.name: Café Alveland or the Andenes bakery
eat.note: Breakfast on the way out of Andøya. Hand-made cinnamon rolls and open sandwiches.

eat.name: Coop Extra, Sortland
eat.note: The mid-trip restock. Bigger and cheaper than anything in Lofoten outside Leknes and Svolvær — and it’s on the road, not a detour.

eat.name: Ekspedisjonen, Sortland
eat.note: Harbourside, modern Norwegian on local fish and lamb. Only if you skip the whale safari and have time to sit — otherwise eat in the van in Lofoten tonight.

eat.name: Arrival dinner in the van
eat.note: Cook at the pitch with the Sortland shopping. First night in Lofoten, sea in front of you, nothing else to do.

drink.name: Vinmonopolet Sortland
drink.note: Last easy wine and spirits stop before Lofoten. Closes 18:00 weekdays — you'll be through around 14:00, so it works.

drink.name: A beer on the north coast
drink.note: The one that tastes like a day you stole.

tips: The trade, plainly: you give up the Vesterålen scenic night and one lazy afternoon, and you gain a Lofoten evening plus an unhurried Friday morning. Sandsletta stays booked for Fri 7 — nothing needs changing.
What you lose: this was the trip's only slack day. Six summits in seven days now has no shock absorber — if the weather turns, something gets dropped rather than shuffled.
You still drive through Sortland, so the big restock, diesel and Vinmonopolet are all unaffected.
Melbu–Fiskebøl takes no booking. Roughly hourly, ~25 min, pay on board — so a late whale safari just means a later sailing, not a missed one.

stops.name: Whale safari, Andenes
stops.note: Sperm whales off the Andøya shelf. It still fits: back around 13:00, ferry at 17:15 instead of 16:15, in Lofoten by 18:30 with four hours of light left.

stops.name: Coop Extra, Sortland
stops.note: The last big, cheap supermarket before Lofoten — and you pass the door. Restock properly here: fresh veg, bread, coffee, beer.

stops.name: Delpen
stops.note: You’re camped on its doorstep tonight instead of tomorrow. The guide calls Delpsheia ‘mandatory if you find yourself in Lofoten’ — 374 m, short and steep, with the iconic look down on Midnattsolveien and the turquoise shallows. Park at the second lot marked ‘Matmora’ on the road to Delp. Muddy and slippery when wet.

blocks.text: No ferry to catch and no Vesterålen to cross — the morning you gained. Coffee on the north coast, or Delpen if you didn’t walk it last night.
Down to Svolvær, the islands’ capital: harbour, Kringla for cinnamon buns, coffee at Brent.

blocks.label: Hike
blocks.text: above town — the classic look down over Svolvær and the Vestfjord.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Settle at Sandsletta Camping (booked ✓); cast from the harbour walls.

deepdive.lead: A day that just got easier: you're already in Lofoten, so the ferry and the long drive are behind you. A slow morning, then Fløya above Svolvær.

route.summary: ~55 km, 1h. Barely a driving day — the crossing happened yesterday.

legs.note: South across Austvågøy to the capital. No queue, no ferry, no rush.
Back north to the booked pitch on the sea.

sleep.name: Sandsletta Camping
sleep.note: Booked ✓. At Laukvik on Austvågøy, on the sea with an open northern horizon — the good one for late light.

hikes.note: Straight up behind Svolvær. The Sherpa steps do most of the climbing; Djevelporten — the boulder wedged in the cleft — is the photo, and it’s a short scramble past the summit. Slippery when wet.

hikes.name: Tjeldbergtind
hikes.stats: 3.8 km · 367 m · 1–2 h · easy, not exposed
hikes.note: The guide's answer to a bad day: Fløya is exposed with a narrow summit, and its Sherpa steps are lethal in rain. Tjeldbergtind is 20 min from Svolvær centre, never exposed, and 'never disappoints'. Take it instead of the galleries if the cloud is down.

eat.name: Børsen Spiseri, Svolvær
eat.note: In an old fish-landing house on the quay. Stockfish, whale, cloudberries — the classic Lofoten dinner. Book it.

eat.name: Kringla bakeri, Svolvær
eat.note: The town bakery — and the place that makes the bun for Anita’s famous fish burger further south. Pastries for the hike.

eat.name: Rema 1000 / Coop Extra Svolvær
eat.note: One of only two proper supermarket clusters in Lofoten (the other is Leknes). Restock properly here.

drink.name: Lofotpils, Svolvær
drink.note: Lofoten’s only brewery, in a 1937 fish-landing facility. The one Norwegian microbrewery that brews pilsner — the Svolvær water is pure enough for it. Tours end in a 6-beer tasting; there’s a food pairing with fenalår, dried whale and cod-roe caviar.

tips: The morning is yours: no ferry, no Vesterålen crossing. If the sky is clear and the legs are fresh, Delpen before you drive south.
Djevelporten and Fløya are one outing, not two: the Sherpa stairs, the boulder in the cleft, then the ridge on to the summit. Fløya is 590 m and exposed with a narrow top — it's the harder half.
Fløya’s steps are unforgiving in rain. If it’s wet, swap it for the galleries and save the legs for Festvågtinden tomorrow.
Svolvær is the last big-shop chance for four days. Beer, coffee, gas.
Sandsletta is booked and paid for tonight (Fri 7 → Sat 8) — the date did not move when you dropped the Sortland night.

stops.name: Kabelvåg
stops.note: The old capital of Lofoten and a prettier harbour than Svolvær — wooden houses round a small bay, plus the Lofotmuseum and the aquarium. Straight past on the way back to Laukvik.

stops.name: Delpen
stops.note: On the doorstep at Laukvik, with an unobstructed northern horizon. Short and steep, not technical. Skip it if you walked it last night — or if Fløya has finished your legs.

blocks.text: early while the small trailhead car park is quiet — a steep, short pull to one of Lofoten's best viewpoints (~2.5–3h).
Drop into Henningsvær — galleries, good coffee, the football pitch out on the rocks, the working harbour. (Lot full? Do the village first, hike later.)
West over the Gimsøy bridges, then the Valbergsveien backroad to Leknes rather than the E10 — quieter, prettier, barely longer.

blocks.label: Surf
blocks.text: Through the tunnel to Unstad and out to the beach car park on Unstadsjøveien — open 24 h, right on the sand, north-facing. Surf, then the low sun down the bay.

deepdive.lead: Festvågtind at dawn, Henningsvær for the afternoon, then west across the islands to sleep at a surf beach instead of on the Gimsøy flats.

route.summary: ~110 km, 2h. Longer than the old Gimsøy night, but it buys a shorter D7 — and the drive west is the Valbergsveien backroad, which was always the plan, just a day earlier.
route.driveTip: Fuel and a shop in Leknes on the way through — Unstad itself has nothing.

legs.note: Trailhead is at the Henningsvær turn-off, right on the E10.
Over the bridges onto the islets. Park at the entrance — the village lanes are tight.
Over the Gimsøy bridges, then take the Valberg/Stamsund backroad instead of the E10. A little beach, a pretty church, no traffic.
Shop and fuel in Leknes first — Unstad has no shop. Then the tunnel, and left down Unstadsjøveien to the car park on the beach.

sleep.name: Unstad beach car park (Unstadsjøveien)
sleep.note: Right on Unstadstranden, open 24 h, 4.4★. Free, and a better view than any pitch inland. Check the signage on arrival — Lofoten has been closing car parks to overnight campers since 2020, and a 24-h parking listing is not a camping permit. If it's signed against, Unstad Arctic Surf is 1 km up the road.
facilities: On the beach — north-facing, for the late light
No services: fill water and empty tanks in Leknes
Café, sauna & board hire 1 km inland at Arctic Surf

hikes.note: Short and steep — 541 m of gain in 2.6 km, so it's straight up. From the top you look down on Henningsvær on its skerries and the whole Vestfjord. Exposed near the summit. Do it before the tour buses.

hikes.name: Heia
hikes.note: The smaller mountain just below Festvågtind — the guide's line is 'the same iconic view over the fishing village for half the effort'. Same trailhead.

hikes.name: Rørvikheia
hikes.stats: 1.5 km · 300 m · 1–2 h · easy but steep, exposed
hikes.note: Straight up from the Rørvika car park you're already stopping at — 300 m for a spectacular look down into that turquoise water. Unmarked path, steep, but short.

hikes.name: Nonstind (Unstad)
hikes.stats: 4.5 km · 483 m · 2–3 h · easy, slightly exposed at the top
hikes.note: Straight off the beach you're sleeping on — walk with the beach behind you, up past Utdalsvatnet, and the summit gives the best view back down on the surf village. The guide's point: it's a gradual incline, so you can turn back whenever and still have won.

hikes.name: Saupstadtind
hikes.stats: 4 km · 367 m · 2 h · moderate, some exposure
hikes.note: Trailhead right after the Unstad tunnel. Views to Unstad, Himmeltinden and the turquoise shallows at Tangstad — and, per the guide, 'off the beaten track, so you won't have to hike in a queue'.

eat.name: Trevarefabrikken
eat.note: A 1948 carpentry works, now the hippest room in Lofoten. Fresh bakes, cold-pressed juice, seasonal sandwiches; wood-fired pizza in high season.

eat.name: Klatrekaféen (the Climbers’ Café)
eat.note: Lofoten's most famous café — fishburger, bacalao, lamb stew, cinnamon buns. Lunch here after the summit.

eat.name: Henningsvær Lysstøperi & Café
eat.note: Candle factory and café in one. Get the Lofotkake — cream and almond paste.

eat.name: Unstad Arctic Surf café
eat.note: On the guide's cinnamon-bun list, and the only food at Unstad — 1 km inland from the car park. Buy dinner in Leknes; treat this as breakfast.

drink.name: Klatrekaféen bar
drink.note: The village's living room. Gigs and DJs most Fridays and Saturdays — and this is a Saturday.

drink.name: Sauna + hot tub, Unstad Arctic Surf
drink.note: Rentable even if you sleep in the car park. Sea, sauna, hot tub, beer, low sun. Ask on arrival — 10% off for Arctic Camper vans.

tips: Read the signs before you settle. This is a car park, not a campsite — and Lofoten has spent five years signposting bans on exactly this. If overnight is prohibited, don't chance it: Unstad Arctic Surf is 1 km inland (email hello@unstadsurf.com · +47 970 61 201), and Brustranda Sjøcamping or Lofoten Camp are the nearest official sites.
No services at the car park. Fill water and empty tanks in Leknes on the way through, and buy dinner there — Unstad has a café and nothing else.

Why the swap works: Unstad is on the guide's midnight-sun shortlist, gives you two new summits off the doorstep (Nonstind, Saupstadtind), puts the Eggum coast path within walking distance, and starts D7 only 30 min from Haukland.

What you gave up: Gimsøy carried Hoven (368 m, the guide's favourite midnight-sun hike) and Lofoten Links golf. The golf was always a swap for the Bleik round rather than a second one — but if Bleik falls through, this is the round you lose with it.
It's no longer a free night. Lofoten has been closing lay-bys to overnight campers since 2020 — don't count on a wild pitch here. Budget kr 200, less the 10% Arctic Camper discount.
Two Unstads. Unstad Camping (Unstadsjøveien) is the beachfront one; Unstad Arctic Surf (Unstadsveien) is slightly inland and has the sauna, hot tub and kitchen. You want the second.
Buy dinner in Leknes. Unstad has a café and nothing else.

stops.name: Rørvikstranda
stops.note: Sits right at the E10 exit to Henningsvær, so you pass the door. Sheltered turquoise bay, light sand, toilets — and quiet, because everyone drives by. Swim after Festvågtinden.

stops.name: Valbergsveien
stops.note: The Gimsøya → Leknes backroad instead of the E10. Quieter, a little beach, a pretty church. It moved here from D7 the moment you chose Unstad — this is the road that gets you there.

stops.name: Coop Extra / Rema, Leknes
stops.note: Unstad has no shop. This is your last chance for tonight's dinner and tomorrow's breakfast — open to 23:00.

stops.name: Unstad viewpoint
stops.note: Just before the final tunnel there's a road on the right up to a viewpoint over the whole bay. Ten seconds off the route, and it's the shot.

stops.name: Unstad Arctic Surf · sauna & hot tub
stops.note: Even if you sleep in the car park, they'll rent you the sauna, the hot tub, a board and a cinnamon bun. Ask when you arrive — and they give 10% to Arctic Camper vans, which is yours.

days.title: Haukland & Uttakleiv

blocks.label: Walk
blocks.text: The Unstad → Eggum coast path from the door, as far as you fancy — or Nonstind above the beach if the legs are fresh.
Claim a spot at the Haukland beach car park (first-come) and let the whole afternoon be yours.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Walk the coastal path over the headland to Uttakleiv — two of Norway's finest beaches, back to back — and fish the shoreline.

days.noteHTML: Camping in nature: vans park in the beach lot (first-come, sometimes restricted) — Uttakleiv's simple self-service pitch over the headland is the backup. Grill on the sand, not the grass.

deepdive.lead: You wake at a surf beach 30 minutes from Haukland. The coast path to Eggum is on your doorstep; the two famous beaches are the afternoon.

route.summary: ~40 km, 55 min. Shorter than it was — sleeping at Unstad did that.

legs.note: Back through the tunnel and over to the west coast. Haukland is the turquoise one with the white sand.
Through the one-lane tunnel under the hill — or walk the coast path over it, which is better.
Small roadside car park on the way back east.

sleep.name: Haukland · scenic night
sleep.note: Designated camper parking at Haukland (paid, ~kr 200, with toilets) — or a free pitch further along. Turquoise water and the low sun straight down the bay.
facilities: Toilets at the beach car park

hikes.note: A small hill with an outsized view — Haukland and Vik beaches below you in one frame, the Vestfjord beyond. 3.7 km, 454 m, steep but short. The golden-hour hike of the trip.

hikes.name: Haukland → Uttakleiv coast path
hikes.note: The proper way between the two beaches, over the shoulder instead of through the tunnel. 4 km each way — walk out and come back over the old trade road.

hikes.name: Veggen
hikes.stats: 3 km · 489 m · 2–3 h · moderate, not exposed
hikes.note: The find of the guide for tonight: 'the less known big brother of Mannen', trailhead right at the Uttakleiv car park, view down onto both Haukland and Uttakleiv, and a summit secluded by the ocean — 'ideal for sunrises, sunsets and midnight sun'. You are sleeping at Haukland. This is the one.

hikes.name: Himmeltind
hikes.when: The big one — only if fresh
hikes.note: The highest mountain on Vestvågøy, from the same Haukland car park as Mannen. Honest note: with Offersøykammen and the coast path already on today, this is a swap for the whole day, not an addition. Read this before you commit: the guide calls it 'a relatively easy climb'. AllTrails does not — it reports extremely steep rocky upper sections with fixed chains and ropes, high exposure, and warns they turn treacherous when wet. Believe AllTrails. This is not a casual add-on.

eat.name: Beach cook-up
eat.note: Haukland has proper picnic spots. Cook at the van, eat on the sand — this is what the camper is for.

eat.name: Himmel og Havn, Ballstad
eat.note: Harbourside in the fishing village 20 min south. Fish soup and the day’s catch, unfussy.

eat.name: Coop Extra Leknes
eat.note: 10 min inland. The other big supermarket in Lofoten — the last cheap restock before Reine. Open to 23:00.

eat.name: Sea-swim + coffee
eat.note: Haukland water in August is about 12 °C. It’s survivable, briefly, and the coffee after is transcendent.

drink.name: Van stock
drink.note: No bar out here — this is a thermos-and-a-cold-one night on the beach.

tips: Uttakleiv is the sunset beach; Haukland is the swimming one. Do Uttakleiv late.
Uttakleiv charges for parking and enforces it. Pay at the machine.
The famous ‘Dragon’s Eye’ rock pool is at Uttakleiv — at the north end, easy to miss.

stops.name: Unstad → Eggum coast path
stops.note: No longer a detour by car — it starts at the beach you slept on. To the right of Unstad beach, a path runs the coast to Eggum. Walk as far as you fancy and turn back; it's the morning, not the day.

days.title: Kvalvika & Ryten → Beach Camp

blocks.text: At the Fredvang trailhead before the parking crunch.
for the iconic clifftop view down onto Kvalvika's wild beach, dropping to the sand before turning back (~4–5h).
Roll into Lofoten Beach Camp on Skagsanden and claim your pitch now — it’s first-come and fills fast, so be in early, not at the 18:00 cut-off. Book your surf session at check-in.

blocks.label: Surf
blocks.text: Surf Skagsanden — board and wetsuit straight from the camp, right on the sand.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Sauna (to 22:00) and a sea dip, then dinner and a beer at the beach bar (to 23:00); cast off the beach in the late light.

days.noteHTML: The spot Lisa flagged: surf · sauna · beach bar on the sand — and a perfect base for tomorrow's Reinebringen.

deepdive.lead: The hike of the trip: over the pass to a beach with no road, and a ledge to see it from.

legs.note: E10 south-west, then over the Fredvang bridges. Trailhead car park is small and fills by 09:00 — paid, card only.
First-come. Get there by ~14:00 to claim a pitch.

sleep.name: Lofoten Beach Camp
sleep.note: No booking — first-come, so time the hike to be back by early afternoon. On Skagsanden beach, which is where the surfers are.

hikes.note: The full loop: over the pass to Kvalvika — a beach reachable only on foot, with the sea coming in hard — then up onto Ryten and out to the ledge above it. 7.6 km, 597 m. Boggy in the middle section, so waterproof boots. The overhang shot is at the very top; the drop is real, so don’t clown about.

hikes.name: Volandstind
hikes.stats: 4.5 km · 457 m · 1–2 h · easy, not exposed
hikes.note: The pointy summit you see from the Fredvang bridge — you drive under it on the way to the trailhead. The guide calls it 'a solid alternative to Offersøykammen, which can be quite crowded'. Looks steep from below; isn't, from the back. Stat clash: the guide calls this easy, 1–2 h; AllTrails rates it hard, 5.1 km / 432 m / 2.5–3 h. Treat it as the AllTrails version and you won't be caught out.

hikes.name: Flakstadtind
hikes.note: The trailhead is on the road to Ramberg, ~500 m from Skagsanden — your campsite. Hands-and-feet scrambly and a tropical view over Flakstad. Realistically: you'll have done six hours on Ryten. Bank it for the morning.

eat.name: Kvalvika lunch
eat.note: Carry it in. Rolls, cheese, chocolate, a thermos. Eating on that beach after the pass is the memory you’re paying for.

eat.name: Beach Camp bar & kitchen
eat.note: Burgers, pizza, beer, right on Skagsanden. After Ryten you will not want to cook, and you won’t have to.

eat.name: Friisgården / Joker Fredvang
eat.note: Small and pricey. Buy what you need in Leknes on the way through, not here.

drink.name: Beach Camp bar
drink.note: Surf crowd, cold beer, the sun going down over Skagsanden. Earned.

drink.name: Sauna
drink.note: The site has one. After 6 hours on Ryten, book it the moment you arrive.

tips: Start early. The Torsfjord car park is genuinely small and the roadside is enforced.

Trailhead, per the guide: you can go Torsfjord → Kvalvika → up to Ryten (the plan — beach first, and the better story), or straight up from Innersand / Fredvang school. The guide recommends the latter for more parking and a more comfortable climb. If the Torsfjord car park is full at 09:00, don't circle — drive to Innersand.
The Kvalvika sand blows hard — the beach is beautiful and often brutal. Take a windproof.
Skagsanden at low tide is a mirror. Bring the camera down at ~22:00.

stops.name: Vikten · Glasshytta
stops.note: Glassblowing in a workshop on the Flakstadøy shore. Usually shuts around 17:00, so it has to be the first stop after Ryten or not at all.

stops.name: Ramberg
stops.note: The big white crescent, straight on the E10 five minutes from Beach Camp. Zero effort — walk it in the evening light.

stops.name: Flakstad kirke
stops.note: The red timber church behind Flakstad beach, same stretch of road. Two minutes, and it frames the mountains.

stops.name: Myrland
stops.note: Down a single-track road past the Napp tunnel. Big boulders sitting in the tideline on white sand — the one people miss.

stops.name: Nusfjord
stops.note: One of Norway's best-preserved fishing villages, 20 min from camp. Charges entry. Honest warning: you'll have done 6 hours on Ryten today — take one or two of these five, not all of them.

days.title: Reinebringen & Reine

blocks.text: before the stair traffic — the Sherpa staircase to the most photographed view in Lofoten (~2.5–3h).
Down by late morning; base at Moskenes Camping.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Drift through the villages at the end of the road — Reine, Hamnøy, Sakrisøy and Å — with deep water for fishing right beside the tarmac. (Optional guided kayak under the granite walls.)

days.noteHTML: Suggested stops: Å at the very end of the E10 — turf-roofed rorbuer, ~5 km on — is an easy stroll to fold into today. For more, the Reinefjord ferry (park at Reine, no cars aboard) crosses to Vindstad for the easy ~1 h hike to Bunes beach: a full day-trip on its own, or camp wild on the dunes overnight. Book the ferry ahead in summer.

deepdive.lead: 1,978 stone steps at dawn, then a slow potter down to where the road ends.

route.summary: ~30 km, 40 min. Barely any driving — the day is the stairs and the villages.

legs.note: E10 south. Trailhead is right on the roadside just past Reine — parking is scarce, go early.
The end of the E10. Literally: the road stops.
Booked.

sleep.name: Moskenes Camping
sleep.note: Booked. By the Moskenes ferry quay, with the Reine peaks behind. Call ~2 days ahead to reconfirm (there’s a reminder in your calendar for 9 Aug).

hikes.note: 1,978 Sherpa-built stone steps up 448 m. It is relentless and it is worth it: the view down onto Reine, Sakrisøy and Hamnøy scattered across the fjord is the picture of Lofoten. Go at dawn — by 10:00 the steps are a queue. Steps are slick in rain and there’s no shelter.

hikes.name: Helvetestind
hikes.note: This upgrades the Bunes plan-B into a real day: the Reinefjord ferry from Reine (~30 min), then Helvetestind straight up from the Vindstad dock — and Bunes beach is a 45-min walk from the same jetty. Check the ferry times before you commit. The AllTrails route pairs it with Brunakseltinden: 9 km / 585 m / 3.5–4 h, rated hard. The Vindstad ferry runs twice a day — one morning, one afternoon. Miss it and you're sleeping on the beach (which, to be fair, is allowed).

eat.name: Anita’s Sjømat, Sakrisøy
eat.note: The fish burger. A seafood shop-bistro in the yellow rorbu village — stockfish, smoked salmon, caviar, and a burger whose bun is baked specially at Kringla in Svolvær. No reservations, just queue. Lunch here, not dinner.

eat.name: Bakeriet på Å
eat.note: Cinnamon buns from a wood-fired stone oven built in 1878, in a bakery from 1844. Open daily 09:00–15:00 in season. This is a pilgrimage, not a snack.

eat.name: Gammelbua, Reine
eat.note: Dinner in an old rorbu — the classic Reine table. Stockfish, fish soup, lamb. Book ahead.

eat.name: Coop Marked Reine
eat.note: Small village shop. Fine for bread and beer, not for a restock.

drink.name: Post-summit beer, Reine harbour
drink.note: You climbed 1,978 steps before breakfast. Sit on the water and enjoy it.

tips: Reinebringen at 06:00 is a different mountain from Reinebringen at 10:00. Set the alarm.
The Reinefjord ferry to Vindstad (for Bunes beach) leaves from Reine — a fine plan B if the summit is clouded in.
Å is the last village on the road and a working museum. The bakery closes at 15:00 sharp.

stops.name: Sørvågen
stops.cost: free · on the way to Å
stops.note: Sits between Reine and Å, so you drive through it twice regardless. Stop once.

blocks.text: Depart east on the E10 — the long road home begins, so cue up the albums.
Break it with a Svolvær viewpoint and a fjord stop or two.

blocks.label: Fish
blocks.text: Find a scenic, free pitch near Narvik or Bjerkvik for a last quiet night by the water — one final cast.

deepdive.lead: The long one. Off the islands, back along the E10, and one last quiet night on the water.

route.summary: ~290 km, 4h30 — the biggest drive of the trip. Cue the albums.

legs.note: Back up the whole archipelago on the E10. Last look at everything.
Over the Lofast bridges and out onto the mainland — the moment the peaks let go.

route.driveTip: Fuel in Svolvær or Leknes. Long empty stretches on the Lofast.

sleep.name: Near Narvik / Bjerkvik · scenic night
sleep.note: A free, scenic fjord-side pitch. Quiet, still water, one final cast.
facilities: No services — empty and fill in Svolvær before leaving

eat.name: Last Lofoten shop, Svolvær
eat.note: Rema 1000 on the way out. Buy dinner and breakfast here — the E10 east has very little.

eat.name: Roadside cook-up
eat.note: Fjord lay-by, stove out, whatever’s left in the fridge. Fewer ingredients, better view.

eat.name: Narvik town
eat.note: If you’d rather not cook: Narvik has ordinary, decent restaurants and a working-town feel after two weeks of villages.

drink.name: Last cast, last beer
drink.note: Line in the water, beer in hand, sun refusing to set. Close the loop.

tips: Break it. A Svolvær viewpoint and one fjord stop turn a slog into a drive.
This is the day the trip changes gear — don’t fight it, put on the long albums.
Watch for tunnels and reindeer on the Lofast; both appear without warning.

blocks.text: An easy drive north with the jagged Lyngen Alps across the fjord; a few coffee-and-photo stops.
Pull into Tromsø Lodge & Camping.
Empty tanks, refuel and repack so the last morning is clean.

deepdive.lead: North along the fjords with the Lyngen Alps across the water, and then the tidy-up.

legs.note: E6 north. Long fjord views the whole way.
E8 with the Lyngen Alps jagged across the fjord to your right — the best-value scenery of any drive on the trip.

sleep.name: Tromsø Lodge & Camping
sleep.note: Booked. On the mainland side, ~10 min from the centre. The practical last night: services, showers, and close to the depot.

eat.name: Coffee-and-photo stops on the E8
eat.note: Half a dozen lay-bys with a Lyngen view. Take three of them.

eat.name: Last van dinner
eat.note: Cook out whatever’s left — the fridge has to be empty for the handover anyway.

eat.name: Or: back into Tromsø
eat.note: Bus in from the campsite for a proper last supper. Fiskekompaniet if you skipped it on night one.

drink.name: Campsite last night
drink.note: The one where you look at the photos.

tips: Do the tidy-up tonight, not tomorrow: empty grey and black tanks, refuel to full, repack, sweep out.
The van must go back full of fuel — the depot charges a premium otherwise.
Check the site for anything left in lockers, under the bed, in the awning.

days.title: Van back 10:00 · fly 15:00

blocks.text: Empty the tanks, repack, and one last camp coffee.
Return the van at Arctic Campers, Kvaløysletta.
Transfer to town/airport (depot → TOS ~25 min); a last harbour stroll if there’s time.
Fly TOS → Copenhagen → Brussels (SK 4453 / 0597), home 19:50.

days.noteHTML: Comfortable: the 10:00 drop leaves ~5 h before the flight.

deepdive.lead: Van back at 10:00, flight at 15:00. Enough slack to not feel it.

route.summary: Depot → airport, ~25 min. Nothing else to drive.

legs.note: Drop 10:00. Full tank, empty tanks, clean-ish.
Transfer via town if you want a last harbour hour.

eat.name: Last camp coffee
eat.note: 08:30, outside, in the cold. The proper way to end it.

eat.name: Harbour stroll + a bun
eat.note: The drop leaves ~5 h before the flight. Kaffebønna on the harbour, one last pastry, watch the boats.

eat.name: Airport
eat.note: TOS is small and expensive. Eat in town first.

drink.name: One for the road
drink.note: Ølhallen opens at 11:00 if you want a bookend to night one.

tips: Fly TOS → Copenhagen → Brussels (SK 4453 / 0597), home 19:50.
Duty-free on the way out is worth it — the aquavit is genuinely better bought here.
Photos: back them up before you fly. Two weeks of Lofoten on one SD card is a bad plan.

stops.label: Tromsø · start / end
stops.note: Land late; two easy nights in town — Fjellheisen and the harbour.

stops.label: Senja · N1
stops.note: Pick up the van, big shop, drive to Senja for a pier-side BBQ.

stops.label: Gryllefjord · N2
stops.note: Climb Hesten for the Segla view, then stage by the ferry quay.

stops.label: Andenes / Bleik · N3
stops.note: Morning ferry to Andøya; beach and mackerel fishing at Bleik.

stops.label: Austvågøy · N4
stops.note: Skip the Vesterålen night: cross into Lofoten a day early and sleep near Laukvik.

stops.label: Svolvær · N5
stops.note: Ferry into Lofoten and hike Fløya & Djevelporten above town.

stops.label: Unstad · N6
stops.note: Beachfront car park at Unstadsjøveien — open 24 h, right on Unstadstranden.

stops.label: Haukland · N7
stops.note: A short summit, then the Haukland–Uttakleiv beach walk.

stops.label: Beach Camp · N8
stops.note: Hike Ryten over Kvalvika, then surf and sauna at the beach camp.

stops.label: Reine · N9
stops.note: Dawn Reinebringen stairs, then potter around Reine and Å.

stops.label: Å i Lofoten · N9 side-trip
stops.note: Museum fishing village at the very end of the E10 — turf-roofed red rorbuer, ~5 km past Reine. Easy same-day stroll.

stops.label: Reinefjord ferry · from Reine
stops.note: MS Fjordkyss from Reine harbour to Kjerkfjord & Vindstad (~25 min). No cars aboard — park at Reine; book ahead in summer via Reis Nordland.

stops.label: Bunes Beach · via Vindstad
stops.note: White-sand beach under Helvetestinden — ferry to Vindstad, then an easy ~1 h hike over a low pass. Wild camp on the dunes behind the beach.

stops.label: Narvik · N10
stops.note: The big drive off the islands east to a fjord spot near Narvik.

campsites.name: Midnattsol Camping
campsites.note: No pitch reservations (cabins only) — self check-in machine. Settle in early on ferry day.

campsites.name: Sandsletta Camping
campsites.note: Booked ✓ & paid · Campcation · pitch w/ electricity · kr 450.

campsites.name: Moskenes Camping
campsites.note: Van pitches book online (Campio); tent pitches can't pre-book in summer. Fills mid-afternoon.

campsites.name: Tromsø Lodge & Camping
campsites.note: Strongly recommend online booking; fills in summer.

ferries.booking: No pre-booking — queue on the quay; campervans queue overnight
ferries.note: Catch the ~11:00 (arrive Andenes ~12:40). Be in the 08:30 queue.

ferries.season: Island route up to ~4/day in summer
ferries.note: Free for your vehicle if you disembark at Værøy. Værøy is a full day (Håheia hike). Exact per-date times: Torghatten live booking picker.

items.label: Camper · all-in, paid
items.sub: incl. Golden day, CV insurance, kit, service & toll fee

items.label: Flights · Brussels ⇄ Tromsø × 2 (est.)
items.sub: SAS via Oslo / Copenhagen · Standard, bags incl.

items.label: Campsites × 7
items.sub: incl. Lofoten Beach Camp ~530 w/ power

items.label: Midnight golf, Bleik × 2
items.sub: green fee kr 250 pp (9 holes) + clubs kr 200 a set

items.label: Beer · supermarket, 2+ pp/day
items.sub: ~40 kr/can vs 90–130 in a bar · wine from home

budget.note: "Per day" averages across 11 days; "per person" splits every line in two. The Tromsø stay is the real figure (€227); flights are still an estimate.

activities.title: Puffin safari, Bleiksøya
activities.textHTML: 90 min among ~80,000 puffin pairs with sea eagles overhead — it leaves from the harbour right by your campsite. Do it the afternoon you arrive. puffinsafari.no

activities.title: Arctic surf
activities.textHTML: The world's northernmost surf scene, right on Skagsanden where you're sleeping — boards, wetsuits, lessons and a warm-up sauna on site, no driving. Book the session at check-in. lofotenbeachcamp.no

activities.title: Kayak Reinefjorden
activities.textHTML: A ~3 h guided paddle under the granite walls, with a free sauna after — a perfect Reine afternoon. reinepaddling.no

activities.title: Midnight golf, Bleik
activities.textHTML: Nine holes on the links between the mountains and Bleik beach, teed off at 22:30 with the sun going down at 22:49 and never getting properly dark. Green fee kr 250 (9) / kr 300 (18) / kr 500 for 24 hours; clubs kr 200 a set, so bring nothing but a jumper. The clubhouse sits a few hundred metres from your pitch. Bleik Golfstrømbane · +47 974 30 312 Status: WhatsApp enquiry sent 14 Jul 2026 — tee time + a left-handed and a right-handed rental set. Awaiting reply.

fromHome: Your nice wine — same tax-free cap whether bought here or at the airport, so bring the bottles you want: up to 4 each (8 total) if you skip spirits. Pad them in the checked bags.
Good coffee & BBQ spices/marinades — dear up north.
Charcuterie + a hard cheese for the first nights (up to 10 kg meat/cheese pp from the EEA).
You each have 23 kg checked — room for a soft cooler bag.
inNorway: Bring vs buy: groceries run ~25% above home (meat & dairy worst, fish barely) — so bring cured meat, hard cheese, coffee & BBQ spices; buy fresh & bulky (produce, bread, beer) locally and catch the fish.
Big shop in Tromsø on Monday (Day 1) — Sunday shops shut. Then Sortland, Leknes, Svolvær.
Beer top-up at supermarkets (~40 kr/can vs 90–130 in a bar); sales stop ~20:00 weekdays, ~18:00 Sat, none Sun.
Fresh produce, bread, dairy — and the fish you catch.

checklist.heading: Fishing
items: Travel rod — Caperlan WXM-500 Travel 2.40 m (4-section, packs for flying)
Mackerel feathers + a few 40–60 g pilks

goodToKnow: No northern lights yet — too bright in early Aug.
Midnight sun just ended; days are ~endless.
The van's tall — watch height-barrier car parks.
Treat hike days as swappable around the weather.
Mobile data: Norway's in EU "roam like at home" — your plan works at home rates.
prep.dutyFreeHTML:
The play · duty-free quota

Bring your own wine from home (up to 4 bottles each if you skip spirits), top up beer at Oslo's arrivals tax-free. At ~2 glasses each a night, 8 bottles lasts ~10 nights, then switch to beer. Buy everything fresh and bulky locally; run the BBQ on free, fresh-caught fish.

Standard mix1 L spirits + 1.5 L wine (2 btl) + 2 L beer · per person
Skip spirits→ 3 L wine (4 btl) + 2 L beer
Beer only→ 5 L beer (15 × 0.33)

Trade "down" only (spirits → wine/beer), never up. You can bring more but must declare it and pay Norwegian duty (steep). Plus NOK 6,000 of other goods each.

fuel: Reliable diesel in the towns: Tromsø, Finnsnes, Andenes, Sortland, Svolvær, Leknes.
Thin stretches: Senja's interior, the far SW (Reine → Å), and the long return.
Many northern pumps are unmanned (card only) and close evenings/Sundays — keep the tank above half.
~21 kr/L; ~10–11 L/100 km → ~3,000 kr for the loop. Check AdBlue.
rainDay: Senja: Tungeneset & Bergsbotn viewpoints from the van; Ånderdalen boardwalks.
Lofoten: Lofotr Viking Museum, the Aquarium at Kabelvåg, Vikten glassblowing, a sauna day.
Anytime: swap a summit for the Haukland–Uttakleiv coast path.
driving.breakdown: Save the 24/7 Arctic Campers number day 1; recovery via Viking / NAF.
essentials: Breakdown: save the 24/7 Arctic Campers number day 1; recovery via Viking / NAF.
Road & weather: vegvesen.no (or 175); yr.no.
Hospitals: UNN Tromsø, Harstad, Stokmarknes, Gravdal/Leknes. Bring your EU card.
Mountains: tell someone your plan; turn back in bad weather. On a trail, dial 112.
driving.lead: Keeping fuel in the tank, a plan for the wet days, and the numbers you hope you never need.

overview.splitNote: Camper, flights & the Tromsø stay are locked in; the rest flexes with what you do (set it on the Budget page).

rail.title: Tromsø · land & settle
rail.sub: Land late; two easy nights in town — Fjellheisen and the harbour.

rail.title: Tromsø → Senja
rail.sub: Pick up the van, big shop, drive to Senja for a pier-side BBQ.

rail.title: Hesten → Gryllefjord
rail.sub: Climb Hesten for the Segla view, then stage by the ferry quay.

rail.title: Ferry → Andenes / Bleik
rail.sub: Morning ferry to Andøya; beach and mackerel fishing at Bleik.

rail.title: Andøya → into Lofoten
rail.sub: Skip the Sortland night — cross the Melbu ferry and sleep in Lofoten.

rail.title: Fløya · Svolvær
rail.sub: A slow gained morning, then Fløya & Djevelporten above town.

rail.title: Festvågtind → Unstad
rail.sub: Festvågtinden and Henningsvær, then west to the beach car park at Unstad.

rail.title: Haukland & Uttakleiv
rail.sub: Coast path to Eggum from the door, then Haukland, Uttakleiv & Offersøykammen.

rail.title: Ryten → Lofoten Beach Camp
rail.sub: Hike Ryten over Kvalvika, then surf and sauna at the beach camp.

rail.title: Reinebringen · Reine
rail.sub: Dawn Reinebringen stairs, then potter around Reine and Å.

rail.title: Long drive → Narvik
rail.sub: The big drive off the islands east to a fjord spot near Narvik.

rail.title: Back to Tromsø
rail.sub: Easy drive north past the Lyngen Alps to a Tromsø campsite.

rail.title: Van back · fly home
rail.sub: Return the van at 10:00 and fly home in the afternoon.

drive.note: D3 & D4 add a ferry on top; D1, D10 & D11 are the long hauls.

hike.note: All rated Hard except Offersøykammen (Moderate). Swap around the weather.

daylightHTML:
~19 h
daylight · early Aug
↑ ~03:50sun barely dips~22:45 ↓

Midnight sun's just ended, but at 69° N the sun only grazes the horizon — long twilight bridges the gap, narrowing to ~17 h by the 14th. Hike late, shoot golden hour near midnight, leave the head-torch in the van.
plans: Sun shops shut — essentials only; big shop after pickup
Hotel done — nothing else to reserve
Camp Mefjord, Mefjordvær · kr 400 · booked ✓
Big grocery shop in Tromsø; diesel at Finnsnes
Barbecue hut + outdoor kitchen on site ✓
Last shop at Finnsnes/Senja; fill diesel
Bring-your-own — grill on the quay/rock, clear of trees
Park near the quay for tomorrow’s ferry queue
Diesel + groceries in Andenes on arrival
Barbecue grills + shared kitchen on site ✓
Midnattsol takes no pitch bookings — first-come, arrive early · Ferry pay-on-board ~kr 1,985 (6–8 m) · be in the 08:30 queue for 11:00 · puffin ~kr 500 pp
Bleik Golfstrømbane · 22:30 tee-off · kr 250 pp + kr 200 clubs · enquiry sent 14 Jul (WhatsApp) — awaiting reply
Scenic pitch near Laukvik, Austvågøy · free — a Lofoten night, gained
Diesel + the big Coop Extra restock in Sortland (on the route)
Melbu–Fiskebøl · ~25 min · roughly hourly · pay on board, no booking
Bring-your-own — gas or grill at the pitch
Nothing fixed. Whale safari from Andenes is the one optional call
Sandsletta Camping · kr 450 · booked ✓ (date unchanged)
Sandsletta booked ✓ & paid · no ferry today — you crossed yesterday

Cinnamon buns at Kringla or Duus Matverk (Svolvær), coffee at Brent. Right by your Sandsletta stop in Laukvik: Kafé Naust, with the Keans Beans coffee distillery next door, plus Livland Gård for homemade ice cream. Boat day → a silent electric Trollfjord cruise or an eagle/orca RIB (Brim Explorer · XX Lofoten), or the ferry over to Skrova island.— from @helenemoo’s Lofoten guide
Unstad beach car park, Unstadsjøveien · free · open 24 h — check the signage
Leknes on the way through — Unstad has no shop
Shared kitchen on site ✓ · sauna + hot tub
Nothing to book. Fallback if it's signed against: Unstad Arctic Surf, 1 km inland (hello@unstadsurf.com · +47 970 61 201)
Valbergsveien backroad (Gimsøya → Leknes) instead of the E10
Big shop in Leknes before the beach
Grill on the sand at Haukland, clear of the grass
First-come; overnighting in the lot can be restricted — Uttakleiv’s basic self-service pitch is the backup

Lofoten bakeri in Leknes on the shop run. Worth the detour to Unstad: the guide crowns Arctic Surf’s cinnamon bun the best in Lofoten (huge — share one), right by the famous surf beach; on the way, Lofoten Gårdsysteri farm shop for its own cheese, and the easy coastal walk or viewpoint at Eggum.— from @helenemoo’s Lofoten guide
Lofoten Beach Camp, Skagsanden · ~kr 480 + 50 power
Stock up in Leknes before Flakstad
Beach bar on site; grill on Skagsanden sand
No camper booking — claim pitch by ~14:00 (fills fast); book surf at check-in

Glasshytta at Vikten — watch glass being blown and grab a cinnamon bun; Ramberg’s wide beach and its roadside viewpoint are close by. Detour south to Nusfjord, one of Norway’s best-preserved fishing villages, with Hansines bakeri and the Landhandleriet café.— from @helenemoo’s Lofoten guide
Fuel/shop in Leknes earlier (limited out west); small shop in Reine
Covered BBQ huts + guest kitchen on site ✓
Book Moskenes van pitch online via Campio (fills mid-afternoon) · optional kayak ~kr 2,200 · Reinebringen 07:30 for parking

Å bakeri claims Lofoten’s best cinnamon buns at the literal end of the road; Anita’s Seafood at Sakrisøy for its famous fishcakes and fish burger; Bringen for coffee in Reine. On the water: SUP and snorkelling at Sakrisøy (Schibevaag) or a fishing/RIB trip from Reine (Aqua Lofoten).— from @helenemoo’s Lofoten guide
Fill up before the long E10; diesel in Narvik
Tromsø Lodge and Camping · ~kr 400–450
Barbecue facilities + common kitchen on site ✓
Book Tromsø Lodge online in advance — fills in summer · empty tanks tonight, refuel before drop
Top up diesel near the depot before the 10:00 return
activityExtrasHTML:
Free & unforgettable

  • A "midnight" summit — Reinebringen or Festvågtind at 21:00–23:00 for golden light and an empty trail.
  • Sauna + sea dip — the Nordic recovery ritual; saunas at Sandsletta (D5), Lofoten Beach Camp (D8) and Reine (D9).
  • Fish straight off the boat — buy the day's cod or halibut dockside at Henningsvær or Ballstad.
  • One full-album drive — the Day-10 run off the islands is made for no-skip listening.

Free fish for the BBQ

Saltwater fishing is free and licence-free — shore, pier, rocks or boat. Your hand-line is all you need. August's easy targets:

  • Mackerel (makrell) — inshore Jul–Sep, off any pier; best on the grill.
  • Coalfish (sei) — everywhere off rocks & harbour walls.
  • Pollock (lyr) — kelp edges near dusk; white, flaky.
  • Cod (torsk) — jig deeper water near bridge currents.

The rules & best stops

  • Handheld tackle only; stay 100 m+ from fish farms.
  • Mind minimum sizes; eat it there (no selling, no big export).
  • Rivers/lakes need a fiskekort — stick to the sea.
Best shore stops

Husøy & Gryllefjord (Senja) · Andenes breakwater & Bleik · Svolvær/Kabelvåg harbours · Sundklakk bridge · Henningsvær & Ballstad piers · Fredvang bridges · Uttakleiv/Eggum · Reine·Hamnøy·Å roadside deep water.
budgetLead: Estimates for 2026 (≈ €1 = 11.2 NOK). Tick what you'll do and switch the view — figures and the chart update live. Camper, flights and the Tromsø stay are already paid.
budgetTableNote: "Per day" averages across 11 days; "per person" splits every line in two. The Tromsø stay is the real figure (€227); flights are still an estimate.
activitiesLead: Add-ons that fit the days you're already on — book the first three ahead, decide the rest on the day.
deepLead: Everything for one day in one place — the drive, the bed, the climb, the food and the beer. Pick a day.
